toolSpec.name: search_faq
toolSpec.description: Search authoritative Weekly Thing FAQ entries. Use first for questions about subscribing, unsubscribing, cost, membership, RSS, schedule, breaks, archive access, search, Thingy, privacy, community, sharing, contact, site tooling, and how the newsletter works.

toolSpec.name: search_archive
toolSpec.description: Hybrid search over Weekly Thing chunks. Default first stop for broad topics, themes, and evidence gathering. Use iteratively — refine the query and year_range based on what comes back. year_range accepts a [start, end] array, a single "YYYY" string, or {"start": YYYY, "end": YYYY}.

toolSpec.name: get_issue
toolSpec.description: Return one full Weekly Thing issue with section structure preserved. Use when you need full context for a specific issue number you already have.

toolSpec.name: get_section
toolSpec.description: Return a named section from one issue (e.g., "Notable", "Briefly", "Featured"). Cheaper than get_issue when you only need one section.

toolSpec.name: find_links
toolSpec.description: Query link metadata in the active source scope by domain, topic/entity, source_kind, link_kind, link_category, target_resolved, and year range. source_kind can be "weekly_thing", "blog", or "podcast" and lets you isolate one corpus even when the active scope is all. link_kind can be "external" or "internal". link_category includes values like "external", "cross_source", "resolved_post", "collection_page", "upload_asset", "malformed_internal", "internal_unresolved", and "internal_site". cross_source means a link between Jamie-owned corpora such as blog to Weekly Thing or podcast to blog. target_resolved true means an internal blog link resolved to a known target post. Does not fetch linked pages. Call without filters to retrieve top external domains. year_range accepts a [start, end] array, a "YYYY" string, or {"start": YYYY, "end": YYYY}.

toolSpec.name: domain_history
toolSpec.description: List every source in the active scope that cited a domain with date and surrounding link context. Optional source_kind isolates "weekly_thing", "blog", or "podcast"; optional link_kind can be "external" or "internal"; optional link_category and target_resolved narrow internal and cross-source cases. Use for "how often / when / in what context did Jamie link to X" questions.

toolSpec.name: corpus_stats
toolSpec.description: Return deterministic inventory statistics for the active source scope: item counts, chunk counts, link counts, newest/oldest item, generated_at, top external domains, link kind/category counts, and blog also_in_issues cross-reference counts. Optional source_kind isolates "weekly_thing", "blog", or "podcast". Use for corpus inventory, freshness, latest indexed date, cross-source coverage, and aggregate-domain questions before searching semantically.

toolSpec.name: latest_content
toolSpec.description: Return the newest indexed issues/posts/episodes deterministically by publish_date. Optional source_kind isolates "weekly_thing", "blog", or "podcast". Optional has_also_in_issues and also_in_issue filter blog posts that were also featured in Weekly Thing. Use for newest/latest/freshness questions; do not use semantic search for those.

toolSpec.name: quote_search
toolSpec.description: Exact substring search across issue bodies. Use to verify whether a specific phrase, name, or product actually appears in the archive — do not infer coverage from related search_archive hits.

toolSpec.name: list_issues
toolSpec.description: Metadata-only issue listing filtered by year, topic, or entity. Use for counts and aggregate pattern questions where body chunks are not needed (e.g., "how many issues mention X").

toolSpec.name: compare_eras
toolSpec.description: Search the same topic in two year windows and return both result sets. Use for two-window before/after contrasts. For three or more windows (early/middle/recent), call search_archive separately with year_range for each. year_a and year_b accept a [start, end] array, a "YYYY" string, or {"start": YYYY, "end": YYYY}.
